report_name: China-Aligned APT Webworm Targets Europe, Using Discord and MS Graph for C2

threat_actors.name: Webworm
threat_actors.description: A China-aligned APT group that has expanded its cyber-espionage operations from Asia to European government entities. The group utilizes sophisticated backdoors like EchoCreep and GraphWorm that leverage legitimate cloud services (Discord, Microsoft Graph API) for C2 communications.
threat_actors.goals: Cyber-espionage, exfiltration of sensitive government documents.

attack_patterns.name: Application Layer Protocol: Web Protocols
attack_patterns.description: The backdoors EchoCreep and GraphWorm use legitimate web services (Discord API and Microsoft Graph API respectively) for command and control, blending malicious traffic with normal enterprise network activity.

attack_patterns.name: Ingress Tool Transfer
attack_patterns.description: The operators deploy their backdoors, EchoCreep or GraphWorm, onto the compromised systems after gaining initial access.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration to Cloud Storage
attack_patterns.description: The GraphWorm backdoor uses a compromised Microsoft account's OneDrive as a dead-drop location for data exfiltration. The group was also observed exfiltrating files to compromised AWS S3 buckets.

attack_patterns.name: Asymmetric Cryptography
attack_patterns.description: The group's C2 traffic is concealed using the standard TLS encryption provided by the legitimate Discord and Microsoft Graph API services.

attack_patterns.name: Obfuscated Files or Information
attack_patterns.description: The EchoCreep backdoor sends encrypted, base64-encoded system information within the body of its POST requests to a Discord webhook.

attack_patterns.name: Proxy
attack_patterns.description: The group uses various proxy tools, including a custom variant of FRP called WormFrp, to tunnel their traffic and obscure their activities.

malware.name: EchoCreep
malware.description: A stealthy backdoor that uses Discord webhooks for command and control. It sends encrypted system information to an attacker-controlled Discord channel and receives commands in return.

malware.name: GraphWorm
malware.description: An advanced backdoor that abuses the Microsoft Graph API and OneDrive for command and control. It uses a compromised OneDrive account as a dead-drop location for tasking and data exfiltration.

tools.name: WormFrp
tools.description: A custom variant of the FRP proxy tool used by Webworm to tunnel traffic and obscure C2 activities.

tools.name: FRP
tools.description: An open-source reverse proxy tool that the custom tool WormFrp is based on.

identities.name: Government of Belgium
identities.description: A government entity in Belgium targeted by the Webworm APT group for cyber-espionage.

identities.name: Government of Italy
identities.description: A government entity in Italy targeted by the Webworm APT group for cyber-espionage.

identities.name: Government of Poland
identities.description: A government entity in Poland targeted by the Webworm APT group for cyber-espionage.

identities.name: Government of Spain
identities.description: A government entity in Spain from which the Webworm group exfiltrated a network infrastructure diagram.

identities.name: Government of South Africa
identities.description: A government entity in South Africa targeted by the Webworm APT group.

infrastructure.name: Discord Webhook C2
infrastructure.description: The EchoCreep backdoor uses Discord's webhook API as a command and control channel, sending and receiving messages from an attacker-controlled channel.

infrastructure.name: Microsoft Graph API C2
infrastructure.description: The GraphWorm backdoor uses the Microsoft Graph API to access a OneDrive account, which serves as a dead-drop for C2 tasking and exfiltration.

infrastructure.name: AWS S3 Exfiltration Point
infrastructure.description: The Webworm group used compromised AWS S3 buckets as an exfiltration point for stolen data, such as a network diagram from a Spanish government entity.

indicators.name: Anomalous Discord Webhook Access
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Discord Webhook Access
id: 9b1d8c3a-7e4f-4b1d-8c3a-7e4f4b1d8c3a
status: experimental
description: Detects outbound network traffic to Discord webhook URLs, which could indicate the use of the EchoCreep backdoor or similar malware for C2 communications.
references:
  - https://www.welivesecurity.com/en/eset-research/webworm-new-burrowing-techniques/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-24
tags:
  - attack.t1071.001
  - attack.command_and_control
level: medium
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri|contains: '/api/webhooks/'
    cs-host|endswith: 'discord.com'
  filter:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\Discord.exe'
      - '\DiscordPTB.exe'
      - '\DiscordCanary.exe'
  condition: selection and not filter
indicators.description: Detects outbound connections to Discord webhooks from servers or non-standard client applications, which is highly suspicious and could indicate C2 activity by malware like EchoCreep.

indicators.name: Anomalous MS Graph API OneDrive Access
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous MS Graph API OneDrive Access
id: 5a2b3c4d-1e2f-4a5b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects network traffic to Microsoft Graph API OneDrive endpoints, which could indicate the use of the GraphWorm backdoor for C2 communications.
references:
  - https://www.welivesecurity.com/en/eset-research/webworm-new-burrowing-techniques/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-24
tags:
  - attack.t1071.001
  - attack.command_and_control
level: medium
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri|contains: '/v1.0/me/drive/'
    cs-host|endswith: 'graph.microsoft.com'
  filter:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\OneDrive.exe'
      - '\explorer.exe'
  condition: selection and not filter
indicators.description: Monitors for unusual processes or scripts making API calls to Microsoft Graph API OneDrive endpoints, which could indicate C2 activity by malware like GraphWorm.

relationships.source_name: Anomalous MS Graph API OneDrive Access
